purpose: Capture the reusable pattern proven by the Guardians stage-five readability work so Platinum can move from hard-coded tuning toward spec-driven execution and ingestion-derived candidate deltas.
reusablePattern: declare a game-spec layer and affected runtime axes before tuning
generate harness-only candidate profiles from the spec delta
measure baseline versus candidates without changing shipped runtime behavior
require visual/contact-sheet evidence before runtime promotion
promote only bounded runtime deltas with rollback signals and refreshed conformance gates
platformExtractionPlan: Introduce a shared validator for game-spec and candidate-profile artifacts.
Allow harnesses to load candidate profiles by id instead of duplicating patch literals in each analyzer.
Make runtime pack contracts expose declarative stage-band/rank rule maps behind the current hard-coded functions.
Keep shipped runtime behavior unchanged unless a promoted candidate profile is explicitly selected by the pack.
Add Aurora as the second consumer after Guardians proves one full spec-delta-to-promotion cycle.
Feed video-ingestion outputs into proposed candidate profiles rather than directly mutating runtime constants.

guardrails: Aurora-specific capture/challenge mechanics must remain declared as Aurora layers, not backfilled into Guardians.
Reference-video ingestion should produce candidate deltas with confidence and human-review needs.
Platform execution must preserve game identity, score ownership, replay metadata, and release-channel boundaries.

nextStep: Build a shared candidate-profile loader and validator, then convert the Guardians readability candidate analyzer to consume the profile contract instead of local candidate literals.
